title: Keep and Organize Receipts
summary: Save and organize purchase receipts for returns, warranties, or tracking spending.
learnerPromise: You will be able to save and organize receipts so you can find them when needed for returns, warranties, or expense tracking.
whyItMatters: Receipts are proof of purchase. Keeping them organized means you can return items, claim warranties, or check your spending without stress.
realLifeUses: Saving receipts during a trip to track your travel budget
Keeping appliance receipts in case something breaks within the warranty period
Storing clothing receipts until you are sure the items fit and you want to keep them
Organizing digital receipts from online orders in one email folder
materialsNeeded: An envelope, folder, or digital storage like email or notes app
result: A simple system for storing receipts so you can find them quickly later.
youWillLearn: Which receipts are worth keeping and for how long
How to store paper receipts so they stay readable
How to save and organize email or digital receipts
How to label or sort receipts by date or category
miniChallenge: Gather any receipts you have from the past 2 weeks. Choose one place to store them and put them there, sorted by date.
steps: Collect all loose receipts from your bag, wallet, desk, or email inbox.
Decide what to keep: large purchases, items with warranties, things you might return.
Choose a storage spot: an envelope, a folder, or a dedicated email folder for digital receipts.
Sort receipts by date or category and label the storage container clearly.
For digital receipts, forward or move them into your chosen folder or app.
completionCriteria: You collected receipts from multiple places into one spot
You chose a storage system that you can keep using
Your receipts are sorted and labeled in a way you can understand later
commonProblems: Paper receipts fade over time — take a photo as backup
Too many receipts feel overwhelming — start with recent ones and high-value items
Forgetting to save digital receipts — set a habit to move them right after purchase
tips: Take photos of important paper receipts as a backup
Keep receipts for electronics and appliances at least until the warranty ends
Review and clear out old receipts every few months to avoid clutter
